message:
feat(err): port err_set_exit pre-exit hook (bd-usntv)

Adds BSD libutil's `err_set_exit(void (*func)(int))` setter that
registers a global callback invoked by err()/errx()/errc()/verr()/
verrx()/verrc() immediately before they call exit(). The hook
receives the exit code that the fatal path is about to use.

Used by daemons and utilities to release locks, flush logs, or
remove pidfiles when a fatal error path is taken.

Implementation:

- New `ErrExitHook` type alias and `ERR_EXIT_HOOK` Mutex<Option<...>>
  cell in err_abi.
- `err_set_exit(func)` installs the new hook and returns the prior
  one (None if no hook was previously set). Pass None to clear.
- All 6 fatal exit paths (err, verr, errx, verrx, errc, verrc) now
  invoke the hook via `invoke_exit_hook(eval)` immediately before
  calling `sys_exit_group(eval)`.

Tests: 2 abi integration tests serialized via ERR_SET_EXIT_LOCK:
(1) cell-management semantics — install/swap/clear all return
the correct prior hook; (2) fork-based test that spawns a child,
installs a hook that writes the eval byte to a pipe, then calls
errx(7); the parent reads the pipe to confirm the hook fired
before _exit and observes WEXITSTATUS == 7.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-6yd","title":"Crash bundle: deterministic evidence + backtrace capture on failure","description":"Critique mapping: #1 (prove; do not hand-wave).

Deliverables:
- When any smoke/fixture fails, capture:
  - backtrace (RUST_BACKTRACE=1)
  - mode + env vars
  - evidence ledger snapshot (TSM repairs/denials)
  - allocator stats snapshot (arena/quarantine)
  - /proc/self/maps excerpt for pointer provenance debugging

Acceptance:
- Bundle generation is deterministic and bounded (no unbounded logs).
- Bundle is sufficient to reproduce the failure locally.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): crash_bundle_test (9 pass) + gate script. Artifact filenames, bounds, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist all validated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:26.930667114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","close_reason":"Crash bundle specification operational. 9 harness tests pass. check_crash_bundle.sh validates evidence snapshots, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","diagnostics"]}
 {"id":"bd-6z3m","title":"syscall: move_mount NULL target pathname should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged move_mount(AT_FDCWD, "/", AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0) fails with EPERM. Add focused ABI parity coverage so this privilege-first path remains explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:44:07.162600221Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:12:14.739269016Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:12:14.736925699Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-6zwpn","title":"PORT: getenv_r (NetBSD re-entrant getenv)","description":"Port the NetBSD libc `getenv_r(name, buf, buflen)` re-entrant environment lookup. Looks up the value of the environment variable `name` and copies it into the caller-provided `buf` (which must hold at least `strlen(value)+1` bytes). Returns 0 on success, -1 with errno=ENOENT if the variable is not set, or -1 with errno=ERANGE if `buf` is too small for the value plus NUL. Useful for callers that want to avoid the global-pointer aliasing risk of plain getenv. Implementation: thin abi shim around the existing getenv() that copies bytes into the caller buffer with bounds checking; tests cover lookup-success, missing-variable, undersized-buffer, NULL-name/buf guards.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:46:08.736953098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:46:08.736953098Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Looks up the value of the environment variable `name` and copies it into the caller-provided `buf` (which must hold at least `strlen(value)+1` bytes). Returns 0 on success, -1 with errno=ENOENT if the variable is not set, or -1 with errno=ERANGE if `buf` is too small for the value plus NUL. Useful for callers that want to avoid the global-pointer aliasing risk of plain getenv. Implementation: thin abi shim around the existing getenv() that copies bytes into the caller buffer with bounds checking; tests cover lookup-success, missing-variable, undersized-buffer, NULL-name/buf guards.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:46:08.736953098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:49:34.296063814Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:49:34.293939872Z","close_reason":"shipped: getenv_r re-entrant getenv with EINVAL/ENOENT/ERANGE error reporting + 7 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add focused ABI regression coverage for the deleted-handle write contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:40:01.361649067Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:45:57.687165842Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:45:57.684868423Z","close_reason":"timer_settime(deleted, &new, NULL) → EINVAL pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-73gu","title":"Metamorphic tests for pthread mutex contract state machine","description":"Add metamorphic relation (MR) proptests to frankenlibc-core::pthread::mutex for mutex_contract_transition. Gaps in existing coverage: (1) TryLock.blocks invariant (must always be false), (2) Lock+Unlock roundtrip identity from Unlocked, (3) blocks=>errno=0 implication, (4) N-cycle Lock/Unlock preserves Unlocked state, (5) Destroy from Locked* returns EBUSY preserving state. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mutex.rs #[cfg(test)] module. One MR per commit, each validated against existing passing behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:03:36.092304396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:11:24.934564282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:11:24.932597224Z","close_reason":"Added 5 independent metamorphic proptests on mutex_contract_transition: (1) prop_trylock_never_blocks (FS×I/C≈20), (2) prop_lock_unlock_roundtrip_is_identity, (3) prop_trylock_unlock_roundtrip_is_identity, (4) prop_blocks_implies_success (structural invariant over full kind×state×op cube), (5) prop_destroyed_absorbs_non_init_ops + prop_uninitialized_absorbs_non_init_ops (trap-state fences). Mutation-validated MR1 by flipping blocks=false→true in LockedBySelf+TryLock+RECURSIVE arm — caught on first run. All 22 pthread::mutex tests pass. Commits: a41297b, 790211a, 0cf160b, e448ca0.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-unbc","title":"syscall: renameat2 invalid flags should override both-null path EFAULT with EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags stay dominant over null-path faults.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:19:53.600277266Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.734480678Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.732334928Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-usntv","title":"PORT: err_set_exit (BSD libutil pre-exit hook)","description":"Port the BSD libutil `err_set_exit(void (*func)(int))` setter that registers a callback invoked by err()/errx()/errc()/verr()/verrx()/verrc() immediately before they call exit(). The hook receives the exit code that err is about to use. Used by daemons/utilities to release locks, flush logs, or close PID files when a fatal error path is taken. Implementation: add a Mutex-protected global cell holding Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(c_int)> in err_abi; expose a setter that swaps and returns the previous value; modify each of the 6 fatal exit sites (err, verr, errx, verrx, errc, verrc) to invoke the hook before sys_exit_group. Tests cover the cell-management contract (set/get/swap), invocation order via a fork-based test that sets a hook writing to a shared pipe before calling errx (parent reads the pipe to confirm hook fired before exit).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:53:33.527097940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:53:33.527097940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
